This [Ontonagon County, Michigan](/wiki/Ontonagon_County,_Michigan "Ontonagon County, Michigan") location article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bergland,_Michigan&action=edit).

is a [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP) and the primary community in [Bergland Township](/wiki/Bergland_Township,_Michigan "Bergland Township, Michigan"), [Ontonagon County](/wiki/Ontonagon_County,_Michigan "Ontonagon County, Michigan"), [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"), United States. It is situated on the north shore of [Lake Gogebic](/wiki/Lake_Gogebic "Lake Gogebic"), the largest natural inland lake on the [Upper Peninsula of Michigan](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula_of_Michigan "Upper Peninsula of Michigan"). Highway [M-28](/wiki/M-28_(Michigan_highway) "M-28 (Michigan highway)") passes through the center of town, leading east 19 miles (31 km) to [Bruce Crossing](/wiki/Bruce_Crossing,_Michigan "Bruce Crossing, Michigan") and southwest 20 miles (32 km) to [Wakefield](/wiki/Wakefield,_Michigan "Wakefield, Michigan"). [M-64](/wiki/M-64_(Michigan_highway) "M-64 (Michigan highway)") leads north from Bergland 17 miles (27 km) to the shore of [Lake Superior](/wiki/Lake_Superior "Lake Superior") and 30 miles (48 km) to [Ontonagon](/wiki/Ontonagon,_Michigan "Ontonagon, Michigan"), the [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat"). Bergland is bordered to the west by the [Lake Gogebic](/wiki/Lake_Gogebic,_Michigan "Lake Gogebic, Michigan") CDP.
["2020 U.S. Gazetteer Files"](https://www2.census.gov/geo/docs/maps-data/data/gazetteer/2020_Gazetteer/2020_gaz_place_26.txt). United States Census Bureau. Retrieved May 21, 2022.

["2020 Decennial Census Redistricting Data (Public Law 94-171)"](https://api.census.gov/data/2020/dec/pl?get=NAME%2CP1_001N&for=place%3A07620&in=state%3A26). *United States Census Bureau*. 2021. Retrieved March 11, 2026.
["Census of Population and Housing"](http://www.census.gov/prod/www/decennial.html). Census.gov. Retrieved June 4, 2016.

**Bergland** is a [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP) and the primary community in [Bergland Township](/wiki/Bergland_Township,_Michigan "Bergland Township, Michigan"), [Ontonagon County](/wiki/Ontonagon_County,_Michigan "Ontonagon County, Michigan"), [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"), United States. It is situated on the north shore of [Lake Gogebic](/wiki/Lake_Gogebic "Lake Gogebic"), the largest natural inland lake on the [Upper Peninsula of Michigan](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula_of_Michigan "Upper Peninsula of Michigan"). Highway [M-28](/wiki/M-28_(Michigan_highway) "M-28 (Michigan highway)") passes through the center of town, leading east 19 miles (31 km) to [Bruce Crossing](/wiki/Bruce_Crossing,_Michigan "Bruce Crossing, Michigan") and southwest 20 miles (32 km) to [Wakefield](/wiki/Wakefield,_Michigan "Wakefield, Michigan"). [M-64](/wiki/M-64_(Michigan_highway) "M-64 (Michigan highway)") leads north from Bergland 17 miles (27 km) to the shore of [Lake Superior](/wiki/Lake_Superior "Lake Superior") and 30 miles (48 km) to [Ontonagon](/wiki/Ontonagon,_Michigan "Ontonagon, Michigan"), the [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat"). Bergland is bordered to the west by the [Lake Gogebic](/wiki/Lake_Gogebic,_Michigan "Lake Gogebic, Michigan") CDP.

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), Bergland had a population of 141.[[3]](#cite_note-Census2020PLLede-3)

The community was first listed as a CDP prior to the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[2]](#cite_note-GNIS-2)
